title: Safety Concern: Product not melting at 40°C or in mouth (Batch 655881 B)
body: I bought this A2 ghee for its health benefits, but I am extremely disappointed and concerned. Despite the high ambient temperature in my city (nearly 40°C), this ghee remains solid and waxy. More alarmingly, it does not melt when kept in the mouth (37°C), which is a basic property of pure ghee. It leaves a thick, waxy coating on the palate that is hard to wash away. This suggests it is not pure milk fat but potentially adulterated with high-melting-point fats like Vanaspati. I am reporting this batch (655881 B) to FSSAI for investigation. Avoid this batch for health reasons.
evidence: this ghee remains solid and waxy

title: Very Disappointing Experience with Anveshan Cow Ghee
body:
I want to share my honest experience with Anveshan Cow Ghee, and unfortunately, it has been extremely disappointing. I wish I can give negative Stars.

Initially, I purchased one-time (non-subscription) Anveshan cow ghee, and that product was genuinely good in quality, aroma, and texture. Based on that positive experience, I decided to start a subscription.
However, after starting the subscription, the quality dropped drastically.
The ghee delivered under subscription is completely liquid, even in Central India winter temperatures (~7°C).

I regularly use another trusted desi cow ghee brand, which solidifies properly under the same conditions.

There is no aroma, no natural ghee smell, and the texture feels unnatural and poor.

It honestly appears adulterated or heavily processed, unlike the original product I bought earlier.

I am not claiming that all cow ghee must be solid, but when:
temperature is low,
aroma is missing,
texture is watery,
and quality is inconsistent compared to the first batch,
…it raises serious doubts about product purity and consistency.
What’s most concerning is that:
The first non-subscription product was good
The subscription product is completely different and inferior
This feels like different quality products are being sent to subscription customers, which is unacceptable.
I have documented the product, uploaded images, and have also sent the ghee for lab testing. I will share the test results soon.

Anveshan seriously needs to work on quality control and transparency.
If subscription customers are receiving inferior products, trust will be lost very quickly.

My advice:
Until Anveshan addresses this issue seriously, I do NOT recommend buying this product, especially via subscription.
Quality inconsistency in food products—especially something as sensitive as ghee—is simply not acceptable.
evidence: it raises serious doubts about product purity

title: No aroma , No Taste , No grany
body: Actually i purchased this ghee after readings its reviews on amazon . But after trying it i found very less Aroma ,no ghee taste after eating , its like eating only oil without taste. No danedar (grainy) ghee its plain ghee. i generally see almost all the ghee types , like from local and other brand ghee they have grainy but this one does not have. i think companies are fooling us in the name of A2 cow ghee and bilona method . i suggest please buy local ghee from tabela . and also if u want to purchase from online then start and try with smallest bottle so that u know actual product vs marketing. For deep thinkers only A1 and A2 are the types of protein found in milk . A1 and A2 only matters in consumption of milk directly , there is no affect of A1 and A2 in curd and Ghee. Ghee are 100 % Fat and there is no protein overall . commercial non A2 brand ghee and A2 brand Ghee are almost same about nutritional value , there is only +(1- 5)% margin to A2 ghee not because of its milk type but its method of preparation , like Bilona Method, also slightly Good Digestion. But the advantage are very minor or in some case its negligible.
evidence: very less Aroma ,no ghee taste after eating

title: Does not seem pure home made ghee like
body: I bought a one litre ghee directly from their website after watching the Anveshan on shark tank .The ghee arrived well in time and was packed properly.But it seemed to be extremely dark in color unlike when i make ghee at home( from a2 cow milk) and also compared to other a2 cow ghee .I still did not complain and wanted to use it.The taste was nothing ghee like, infact it is completely tasteless and odorless. Also my biggest surprise is that this ghee does not change texture with chnages in room temperature.I am not sure why, since my home made ghee is actually melting in this heat at 32- 35 degrees Celsius. But this one doesn't melt even one bit. While using also ,there are weird thick particles which seem to me that ghee is not separated properly while making.Then i tried to heat it on gas and it turned into a very dark yellow colores liquid with bubbles on top ,but believe you me, no aroma at all.Not sure now what this is, but not the form of ghee I was expecting.
evidence: the taste was nothing ghee like, infact it is completely tasteless

title: A2 but not that great
body:
We purchased this ghee looking for value-for-money options in A2 ghee. Purchasing a smaller bottle, we thought of trying out before we got a larger one. Unfortunately it did not have the same finesse like our earlier purchases of another brand.

Add to that my family and me had slight stomach cramps after consumption. If you are looking for the purpose of tadka or tempering your food, then this is a good choice. Eating it as raw on your cooked rice is not such a great idea.

Perhaps we have sensitive stomachs thus faced this problem. This was just our take on the issue. This may not be applicable for many.

But if you are as finicky as my wife is for taste, then don't go for this one. If you are looking at cooking certain foods with larger spoons of ghee in it, like bottle guard (doodhi) or certain dishes, then this is a good go in being satisfied by A2 to your diet. Hope this helps..
evidence: did not have the same finesse

title: Well Marketed Ghee that need not reflect the ethos
body:
I am writing this review after using this Ghee for almost a month on daily Basis. I have consumed 500gms personally. My way of consuming is mostly with Chapatis or Rice. I have spent my childhood in village and I know what real Ghee tastes like. I used to have handful of ghee sometimes if I feel like eating something and don't have anything available other than Ghee. That is my love for Ghee. And that was the reason I was looking for a real Ghee no matter whats the cost.

Few points I want to add here:
1. The pricing: For a pure Ghee made using Bilona method, the price is too cheap. Desi cos Milk costs around Rs50 per liter to the farmer himself. Now , considering 3-3.5% of fat in a liter of ghee. we need 28-32 liters of milk on an average to make a liter of Ghee.
so, cost of the milk is coming more than Rs1500. Now considering Ghee making cost, amazon's fee, branding packaging, ... providing a hee for Rs1700 is not possible unless they are bearing losses.
Or, What makes more sense is, they are not actually using Raw milk to make Ghee, rather using cream of the Milk. This can explain the cost.

2. The Texture: Texture of the Ghee is very strange, I havenever seen such Ghee which is always liquid no matter whats the ambient temperature. Its grainy which is considered good , but the liquid part of it does seems strange as mentioned in other reviews also.

3. Smell and Taste: Smell and Taste is good in the beginning when you are having your food, but it does fades away soon, if you want to enjoy the taste, you may feel like adding more ghee on your Rice or Cghapati, which is very unusual, as a spoonful of village ghee is enough to make a room filled with aroma and tastes stays on your bud thoruought your food.

4.The Packaging: As I said earlier, The Branding they have done is very good and thus the packaging. But the lid of the Jar is very loose and does not fit well. You can easily miss it sometime if you hold it from the Lid/top that we normally do. You have to be extra careful handing it. I suggest this can be improved.

5. Over all review: I am not going with this Ghee again as I did not find it worthy of the price nor it reflects the taste and ethos of village tradition as marketed by them.
evidence: the lid of the Jar is very loose

title: Feel Disappointed
body: I bought 4* 250 ml from flipkart and 1 * 500ml from amazon within a month... And got Diff qualities of same product.. The first 2 bottles were good and the cap color was black. It was thick, no watery, tasted good, smell was OK.. But then the other 3 bottles which had green caps are watery, no smell and no good taste... For a person who grew up eating pure homemade cow ghee the standards are still nowhere but atleast we should get what we are paying for... I have also made ghee myself from not so original kind of cow milk, even some times packet milk added... Still when I made it making butter first, the ghee came always so textured and being creamy when cooled down, and the ones that I used to make directly from Malai, they never condensed... They are giving all these certificates and all and claiming it to be bilona churned from curd, but it doesn't seem, smell or taste so.... Paying higher price and still being cheated, this is not good...i m so frustrated about all these things today, people charge such high prices online and we still pay hoping to get some genuine and reliable products but still what we get, only deception.... If u really want to make a name out there for ur company, pls stop fooling around...

title: Aroma, Texture and Taste below expectation.

But of late I find Hallikar or Gir cow ghee is being marketed with a yellow shade translucent wrapping around, suggesting caution. To me, the present mild aroma and texture are no match to the age-old homemade Bilona buffalo ghee. Probably the clue lies in the heating and cooling process.

title: Authentic Taste & Premium Quality
body:
I recently tried Anveshan A2 Cow Ghee, and it has quickly become a staple in my kitchen. The aroma is rich and earthy, reminding me of traditional homemade ghee. The texture is smooth, golden, and melts beautifully, making it perfect for cooking, frying, or even adding a spoonful to warm milk.

What I Loved:
Authenticity: Made from A2 milk of grass-fed cows, which gives it a distinct flavor and purity.

Taste: Adds depth to Indian dishes like dal, rotis, and sweets. The nutty fragrance elevates even simple meals.

Health Benefits: Easy to digest, supports immunity, and feels lighter compared to regular ghee.

Packaging: Comes in a neat glass jar that preserves freshness and feels premium.
evidence: The aroma is rich and earthy
